dialogueChunks.intro: The betrayed received the full accounting of who wronged them. Their council sent a one-word reply: 'Begin.'
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} traveled personally to negotiate new terms. The old alliance is dead, but something more honest is growing in its place. Every term is public, every obligation mutual. {quest_ally} says it's slower and harder than the old way. Also more durable.
dialogueChunks.outro: Trust built on truth survives what trust built on convenience cannot, {player_name}. You pulled the rot out by the root.

dialogueChunks.intro: The council confronted the traitor with the evidence. They didn't deny it. Just set down their badge and sat in silence.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They said the {enemy_type_plural} threatened their family. That the first deal was survival and every deal after was too late to stop. {target_npc} doesn't know whether to believe them. {quest_ally} has taken interim command while trust is rebuilt.
dialogueChunks.outro: Whether the traitor was a victim or a villain, the oath they broke put lives at risk, {player_name}. The new procedures require dual authorization. No single point of betrayal.

dialogueChunks.intro: The seal is back. The original document is authenticated. The evictions are null and void. Those families are staying in their homes.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The forger was found trying to sell the seal. {target_npc} has them in custody. The family that benefited from the forgery is claiming ignorance, but the evidence {quest_ally} compiled suggests otherwise.
dialogueChunks.outro: A community's word is only as good as the people who keep the records, {player_name}. You made sure the truth was louder than the lie.
